keyRules: Maximum altitude: 120 m (400 ft) above ground level (AGL)
Drones weighing less than 25 kg: DO NOT require DINACIA registration for recreational use
Drones weighing ≥25 kg or used for commercial purposes: mandatory registration on the DINACIA UTM platform
Always keep the drone within direct line of sight (VLOS)
Do not fly over densely populated areas (cities, towns, neighborhoods)
Avoid airports, airfields, and controlled airspace (CTR)
A reasonable distance from people and buildings not involved (recommended: 30 m)
Flight permitted only during the day — night flights require specific authorization
Business operation: mandatory liability insurance + DINACIA permit
Airspace Reservations (Events): Complete the form 10 days in advance
importantNotes: 🌟 URUGUAY IS PERMISSIVE: DINACIA Resolution 291/2014 states that drones weighing less than 25 kg do NOT require registration for recreational use. Most consumer drones (DJI Mini, Mavic, Air, Phantom) are well below this threshold—but all other operating rules DO apply.

📋 DINACIA RESOLUTION 291/2014: classifies drones into three categories—Small (≤25 kg), Medium (25–260 kg empty weight), and Large (>260 kg). The Uruguayan Aeronautical Regulations (RAU 91 and others) supplement the regulatory framework.

✈️ FOREIGN TOURISTS: The rules also apply to non-residents. For recreational use of a drone weighing less than 25 kg, registration is not required. For commercial/professional use or events: contact DINACIA before your trip. Customs may require you to declare your equipment upon entering the country.

🏖️ PUNTA DEL ESTE AND MONTEVIDEO: Punta del Este (high tourist density), Carrasco Airport (MVD), Laguna del Sauce Airport (PDP), the Government House, and Plaza Independencia have permanent restrictions. Coastlines and beaches tend to be crowded during peak season.

🌳 PROTECTED NATURAL AREAS (SNAP): The National System of Protected Areas includes areas such as Esteros de Farrapos, Cabo Polonio, and Quebrada de los Cuervos. Check with the Ministry of the Environment for specific restrictions before operating drones in these areas.

primaryLinks.title: DINACIA — Basic Regulations for Drones
primaryLinks.description: DINACIA's official website with the basic guidelines for drone operations (Resolution 291/2014): regulations, categories, and authorized operations

primaryLinks.title: UTM DINACIA — Registration and Authorization Platform
primaryLinks.description: DINACIA's official UTM (Unmanned Traffic Management) platform for registering drones (≥25 kg) and submitting applications for operating authorization. Requires a registered user

primaryLinks.title: DINACIA — RPAS Operator Permits
primaryLinks.description: Official page on RPAS operator permits — steps, requirements, and forms. Required for drones weighing 25 kg or more and for all commercial uses

primaryLinks.title: DINACIA — LAR 61 Pilot Licenses
primaryLinks.description: LAR 61 (Pilot Licenses and Ratings) — regulations applicable to aviation operations, including RPAS

secondaryLinks.title: DINACIA — Home Page
secondaryLinks.description: Main website of the National Directorate of Civil Aviation and Aeronautical Infrastructure. Access to procedures, regulations, and aeronautical services

secondaryLinks.title: Procedure: Aviation Licenses (gub.uy)
secondaryLinks.description: Official procedure on gub.uy for the issuance and authorization of aviation licenses — including remote pilots for professional drone use

secondaryLinks.title: AUD — Uruguayan Drone Association
secondaryLinks.description: Uruguayan Drone Association: a community of pilots, educational resources, and updates on regulations applicable to operators in Uruguay

fields.disclaimerText: Always check DINACIA’s official sources before flying. This app is a tool, not a legal authority. Uruguay enforces DINACIA Resolution 291/2014 and the Uruguayan Aeronautical Regulations (RAU). Drones weighing less than 25 kg do not require registration; all others do.
